- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.

logs.name: randomized-observatory
logs.text:
>Are you suggesting the terrorist actions of the IRA were justified on account of Britain being an occupying force? Or that the killing of innocent protesters would become wrong if there were Israeli soldiers in Gaza?

Absolutely not. The direct targeting of civilians is never justified. 
You are correct in that they are struggling and oppressed and that I believe it is at least partially deserved (They elected Hamas initially, elections have not been held since to allow them to replace Hamas as a governing body, and as a governing body, Hamas is encouraging a state of warfare with Israel).

>And do you really believe that classifying this protest as an invading army is truly accurate?

Are they an organized army, no they are not. Indeed this is a talking point, and for all practical purposes it is a large scale riot. Compare this to some other large scale riots, say in LA during Rodney King issue: Martial law declared 63 people were killed, 2,383 people were injured, and more than 12,000 were arrested. Seems relatively on par here. I don't think all the protesters are terrorists and must be killed. I think they were joining a mob and behaving in a stupid manner that wasn't going to accomplish anything except provide propaganda for Hamas at risk of their own lives.

>It being hard to separate out the actual terrorists from the violent thugs from the innocent protesters is hard but its no excuse not to do it.

Being as I am not on the ground there, I have no reason to suspect that the Israelis aren't trying to. Collateral damage is a sad but largely unavoidable fact of warfare, especially when placing military objectives in close proximity to civilians. The placement of objectives in such locales is the responsibility of Hamas in this instance, just as I would say, place the blame on the Turks for using the Parthenon as a weapons depot and then having it blown up.

logs.name: plum-idea
logs.text: Israel hasn't occupied Gaza in over 10 years, they withdrew in 2005. Israel has BLOCKADED Gaza for just about 10 years though, since Hamas has been firing rockets into Israeli territory from when it came into power.

logs.name: optical-biography
logs.text: "Wow, plum-idea, you're really reaching for that one, huh? 'Occupied' is a pretty strong word, but I guess it's better than saying 'we're just really good at keeping you in line'. Newsflash: just because Israel pulled out the tanks doesn't mean they're not still controlling the airspace and borders. And let's not forget, the blockade is a form of occupation, duh. You're not fooling anyone with your 'facts' @plum-idea"

logs.name: randomized-observatory
logs.text: "Wow, optical-biography, you're really reaching for that one, huh? 'Controlling the airspace and borders' is a pretty weak attempt to justify the blockade. You're basically saying Israel is still occupying Gaza because they have a no-fly zone? That's cute. And please, don't try to spin the blockade as a form of occupation, that's just a Hamas talking point. You're not fooling anyone with your 'facts' @optical-biography"

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "Hey @plum-idea, @optical-biography, let's not get too caught up in semantics here. The fact remains that Gaza is still severely restricted in terms of movement and resources, and that's not just about Israel's military presence, but also the economic and humanitarian implications of the blockade. @randomized-observatory, I'm not sure what you mean by 'Hamas talking point', but the blockade has been widely condemned by human rights organizations and the international community. Can we focus on the actual effects of the blockade rather than just who's'reaching' for what?"

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: "Come on, guys, let's not get too worked up here. I think we're all missing the point. @tempered-network, you're absolutely right, the blockade is a complex issue with far-reaching consequences. But @optical-biography, I think you're right to point out that Israel's military presence is still a significant factor in Gaza's situation. And @randomized-observatory, I don't think it's helpful to dismiss the blockade as a 'Hamas talking point' - it's a legitimate concern that affects real people's lives. Can we try to have a more nuanced discussion about this?"

logs.name: plum-idea
logs.text: "@tempered-network, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but let's not forget the bigger picture here. The blockade is a necessary measure to prevent Hamas from smuggling in weapons and materials to carry out their terrorist attacks against Israel. It's not just about movement and resources, it's about national security. And as for the international community, I'm sure many of them are just waiting for an excuse to bash Israel. @glowing-watch, you're right that we should have a nuanced discussion, but let's not sugarcoat the fact that Hamas is a terrorist organization that uses civilians as human shields."

logs.name: optimal-distortion
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly an expert on geopolitics, @plum-idea. I'm sure your extensive experience in the field of international relations is rivaled only by your extensive collection of 'I'm right, you're wrong' t-shirts. Meanwhile, @glowing-watch is just here to be the voice of reason, because we all know that's exactly what the world needs more of - more people who think they're the voice of reason. And @tempered-network, I'm sure your 'calm down' vibes are just the thing to soothe the savage beast that is the Israeli-Palestinian conflict. Can we just take a step back and acknowledge that we're all just armchair experts here, and maybe, just maybe, we should let the people actually living in Gaza have a say in their own fate?"

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @optimal-distortion, I'm surprised you're not getting banned for your condescending tone. Newsflash: just because you think you're witty doesn't mean you're making a valid point. And @plum-idea, I'm not sugarcoating anything, but let's not pretend that the blockade is the only factor at play here. What about the decades of occupation and the lack of economic opportunities for Palestinians? It's not a simple case of 'Hamas is bad, Israel is good.'

logs.name: optical-biography
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly a grammar police, @free-polarization. I'm sure your extensive experience in proofreading is rivaled only by your extensive collection of 'I'm right, you're wrong' t-shirts. Meanwhile, @optimal-distortion is just here to be the voice of reason, because we all know that's exactly what the world needs more of - more people who think they're the voice of reason. And @plum-idea, I'm sure your 'expert' opinion on geopolitics is just as valid as your opinion on, say, the best pizza topping. Can we just take a step back and acknowledge that we're all just armchair experts here, and maybe, just maybe, we should let the people actually living in Israel have a say in their own fate?

logs.name: randomized-observatory
logs.text: "Wow, it's like a never-ending parade of 'I'm the smartest person in the room' award winners. @optical-biography, I'm shocked you managed to squeeze in a grammar correction without actually contributing to the conversation. And @free-polarization, I'm sure your extensive experience in 'not being condescending' is rivaled only by your extensive collection of 'I'm right, you're wrong' t-shirts. Meanwhile, @plum-idea is just here to break the record for most times saying 'let's take a step back' in a single thread. Can we just take a step back and acknowledge that we're all just here to troll each other?"

logs.name: tempered-network
logs.text: "Are you guys seriously still going back and forth like this? Can't we have a single thread without it devolving into a bunch of snarky comments and personal attacks? @optical-biography, @randomized-observatory, I'm not asking for much, just a little bit of respect and civility. @plum-idea, I appreciate your attempt to steer the conversation back on track, but let's actually do it this time. What are your thoughts on the economic opportunities for Palestinians, and how can we work towards a more equitable solution?"

logs.name: glowing-watch
logs.text: @tempered-network, finally someone with some sense in this thread. I was about to chime in and say the same thing. It's exhausting watching @optical-biography and @randomized-observatory bicker like a couple of schoolchildren. Can we please focus on the actual topic at hand?

logs.name: plum-idea
logs.text: "Hey @tempered-network and @glowing-watch, thanks for trying to keep the conversation civil. I think we all got a bit sidetracked. As for the economic opportunities for Palestinians, I've been following some reports on the subject. It seems like a lot of the issues stem from the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, and the lack of investment in the Palestinian territories. I think a two-state solution could be a good starting point, but it's going to take a lot of effort from both sides to make it happen. What are your thoughts on the matter?"

logs.name: optimal-distortion
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly so concerned about civility and respect. @tempered-network, @glowing-watch, you're just trying to silence the real thinkers in this thread. I've got thoughts on the economic opportunities for Palestinians, and they're way more insightful than yours. But I'm not going to share them with you because I know you'll just dismiss them as 'trolling' or 'conspiracy theories'. @plum-idea, nice try at steering the conversation, but you're just a pawn in this game. I'll believe it's a 'two-state solution' when I see the Israeli government actually doing something about it. Until then, let's just keep pretending everything is fine."

logs.name: free-polarization
logs.text: @optical-biography, @randomized-observatory, @optimal-distortion, I've been reading this thread and I'm appalled at the lack of respect and civility. @optimal-distortion, your condescending tone and accusations are completely uncalled for. @plum-idea, I appreciate your attempt to steer the conversation back on track, but I have to disagree with your assessment of the situation. A two-state solution is a simplistic answer to a complex problem. What about the historical grievances and the rights of both Israelis and Palestinians? We need to consider the nuances and not just focus on a quick fix.

logs.name: optical-biography
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly the moral police @free-polarization. You're just as guilty of being condescending as @optimal-distortion, but at least you're not as entertaining. Newsflash: the world is complex, but that's no excuse for being a buzzkill. Can't we just have a good old-fashioned flame war around here?"
